mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in positive direction over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months when CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in PAYEMS exceeding 200,000 in a single month signals an abrupt labor market contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 200000 in a single month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following a >200k PAYEMS drop, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST indicates robust consumer spending and housing demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in >55% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns during economic expansions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months when INDPRO crosses below its trend, or the mechanism shows identical predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in FEDFUNDS exceeding 0.25% in a single meeting cycle shocks short-term funding costs, triggering immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25 in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances following a >0.25% rate hike, or the signal's Rank IC is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS indicates a deteriorating labor market that suppresses consumer income and corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields positive forward returns in >58% of quarters, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested against a single-series baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding GDPC1 reflects growing aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters when GDPC1 shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in INDPRO following a prolonged contraction signals supply chain normalization that relieves cost pressures and improves margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during recovery phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 0.5 after 3 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive edge when isolated from concurrent monetary policy announcements.
